name: Candied Peels
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the oranges in half lengthwise and remove the fruit.
2. Cut the peels into strips.
3. Cook the peels in boiling water for 15 minutes. Drain and repeat.
4. In a large pot, cook the sugar, corn syrup, and 1-1/2 cups water over high heat until the sugar is dissolved.
5. Add the gelatin and peels to the mixture and simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Remove from heat. On a sheet of waxed paper or microwave-safe paper, sprinkle 1 cup granulated sugar.
7. Lightly roll the peels in the sugar, one at a time, until they are evenly coated. Add more sugar as needed.
8. Place the peels in a single layer on waxed paper to dry overnight.
9. Store in an airtight container. They will keep indefinitely if stored dry.
10. Lemon or lime peels may be used with corresponding gelatin, but I have never tried it.
11. For slightly less sweet peels, omit the corn syrup and increase sugar to 3 cups.
